name: Fig Newton Bars
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Brown sugar - dark, firmly packed
1/2 cup Brown sugar - light, firmly packed
3 cups Figs, fresh, finely chopped (see note)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift flour, salt, and cinnamon together.
2. Cream butter and sugars until very fluffy; beat in egg whites and vanilla.
3. Slowly work in the flour mixture; wrap dough and chill for 2 to 3 hours.
4. Meanwhile, prepare filling: Simmer water, sugar, and lemon juice with chopped figs for 5 to 7 minutes until thickened. Cool but do not chill.
5. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
6. Roll out dough a small portion at a time to about 1/4 inch thickness. Cut into pieces about 2-1/2 inches wide and 3 inches long.
7. Place a level teaspoon of fig mixture in the center of each piece. Fold dough around filling, folding as if folding a business letter.
8. Flatten cookies slightly and place seam side down about 1 inch apart on ungreased baking sheets.
9. Bake for about 12 minutes until lightly browned and just firm. Cool on racks.
Note: If fresh figs are not available, substitute 2 cups finely chopped dried golden figs and increase water to 1 cup.
